summary: Iteration 4 — verified Script Studio target_language wiring + Open-in-Crew handoff. Backend: 4/4 pytest pass. (1) POST /api/genius-prompt/generate with target_language='id' returns response.language='id', evolution>=1, and genius_prompt body contains ≥3 Indonesian stopwords (yang/dan/untuk/dengan/...) — heuristic confirms Bahasa Indonesia. (2) Saved Indonesian prompt is retrievable via GET /api/genius-prompts. (3) target_language='en' sanity check: EN stopwords dominate ID stopwords → parameter is actually wiring through. (4) POST /api/script/generate with target_language='id' + saved genius_prompt_id returns a Bahasa Indonesia script. Frontend: Studio→Crew handoff fully verified end-to-end. Filled topic 'Tips produktif kerja remote' with language=id, clicked Generate (script populated in ~25s), clicked Evaluate (card rendered with overall_score 6.2/10), clicked open-in-crew-original-btn → app navigated to /crew, localStorage 'peter_ai.crew_handoff' was written with brief length 2134 starting with 'Build the production checklist', containing '=== SCRIPT ===' and '=== END SCRIPT ===' markers and source='studio', topic='Tips produktif kerja remote', platform='tiktok'. After CrewView mount the localStorage key was CLEARED (lazy useState initializer consumed it on first paint), and the crew textarea (data-testid='crew-requirements-input') was pre-filled with the brief (length 1967). Note: open-in-crew-original-btn is enabled even when overall_score<8 by design (just styled secondary), so the navigation+localStorage handoff still works as expected.
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/genius-prompts/{id} (server.py:879) does NOT include the 'language' field in its response, even though the field IS persisted on the document and IS returned by the POST endpoint. Consider adding 'language': d.get('language') to the detail payload so clients (and tests) can filter Indonesian/English prompts without inspecting the prompt body.

script_studio.py:316 early-stop condition only triggers AFTER iteration 2 (len(history)>=2). With the new iterations=1 default this branch is dead code. Fine for now, but if you ever raise the default, double-check the plateau threshold (0.2) still makes sense.

StudioView.js:178 — eligibleForCrew is computed but the open-in-crew-original button is enabled regardless (only disabled when !script). The visual gold treatment vs. secondary border treatment is the only signal to users that score is <8. Good UX choice for this iteration.

CrewView.js lazy initializer (lines 77-89) correctly avoids the react-hooks/set-state-in-effect ESLint rule and survives Strict-Mode double-invocation because the consume step (localStorage.removeItem) is idempotent. Verified key gets cleared after mount in browser test.

Genius-prompt LLM loop with iterations=1 still takes 50-110s in practice and occasionally trips the ~100s proxy cap with 502. Backend test uses a 1-retry helper + module-scoped fixture that reuses a previously-saved Indonesian prompt (heuristic match on stopwords) to keep CI stable. Not a regression — just flakiness inherent to LLM latency.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (original-script handoff verified end-to-end; variant >=8 button is conditional and the natural evaluation returned 6.2 with no variant ≥8, so that conditional UI was not separately exercised — code path is identical to the verified original-script flow via the same sendToCrew() helper)

seed_data_creation: Several Bahasa Indonesia genius-prompts already exist in db.genius_prompts from prior runs; fixture reuses them via the GET /api/genius-prompts list + heuristic ID-stopword match on the prompt body.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=$(grep REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL /app/frontend/.env | cut -d= -f2) pytest /app/backend/tests/test_studio_language.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_iter4.xml. The id_genius fixture is module-scoped and will reuse a saved Indonesian prompt from prior runs (detected via ID-stopword heuristic on the prompt body) — if the DB is wiped, the fixture falls back to a fresh POST which costs ~60-110s. For the frontend handoff test, do NOT rely on overall_score>=8 to gate the click — the button is enabled at any score (styled secondary below 8). To capture the localStorage handoff value before CrewView consumes it, hook localStorage.setItem in window context before clicking the open-in-crew button (see script in iter4 run). The variant-level open-in-crew-${V}-btn only renders when that variant's projected_score>=8 — to exercise it, you'd need an LLM evaluation that returns variant ≥8.0, which is non-deterministic.
rca of the issue: No bugs found — both requested features (target_language wiring + Open-in-Crew handoff) work as designed.
